title: WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM - 211info
text: NEWBERG WIC WOMEN, INFANTS, AND CHILDREN SUPPLEMENTAL NUTRITION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Public health program designed to improve health outcomes and influence lifetime nutrition and health behaviors for women, infants, and children. Nutrition education classes are provided as part of the program; other services include breastfeeding support, monthly benefits for supplemental nutritious foods, and referrals to community resources. 2251 E Hancock Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Applicants must meet four criteria to be eligible for WIC: -Live in Oregon (US citizenship is not required) -Be a pregnant, postpartum, or breastfeeding woman, or an infant or child under age 5. Fathers, foster parents, and other guardians can also apply on behalf of a qualifying child. -Have a household income below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level (FPL). Families receiving OHP, SNAP, or TANF are automatically income-eligible for WIC. -Have a nutritional risk (assessed at time of appointment) Hours: Monday-Wednesday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm, Thursday 8am-noon and 1pm-6pm, Friday 8am-noon and 1pm-5pm (503) 538-8779 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 538-8779 INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information or appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: Proof of ID for parent or guardian and child, proof of address, and proof of income LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Marion and Yamhill Counties Services: WIC If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. NEWBERG WIC
